title: GoHighLevel July 1 Updates: Prospecting Auto-Enriches Contacts and Finds Decision Makers, WhatsApp Comes to Bookings
desc: GoHighLevel's July 1 changelog turns Prospecting into an enrichment engine with website scraping and POC discovery, plus WhatsApp booking notifications.
keywords: gohighlevel prospecting enrichment, gohighlevel july 2026 updates, gohighlevel poc discovery, gohighlevel whatsapp notifications, gohighlevel rentals, gohighlevel for agencies
cta: Want your GoHighLevel account working this hard?
body:
July 2, 2026. GoHighLevel shipped a quiet but useful batch of updates on July 1, and the headline for agencies is that the built-in Prospecting tool now behaves like a lightweight data enrichment engine. Prospects are no longer added as bare business records pulled from Google Business Profile data; the platform can now scrape a prospect's website for phone numbers and emails and go looking for the actual decision makers. Alongside that, WhatsApp arrived as a notification channel for the Rentals booking suite. None of this made keynote news, but for agencies running local lead generation on HighLevel, it changes the default quality of every record entering the CRM, per the HighLevel changelog.

What shipped on July 1

  1. Prospecting contact enrichment. In sub-account prospecting, where available, the system now automatically enriches prospects by scraping their phone numbers and email addresses from their website, so records arrive outreach-ready instead of half-empty.
  2. Points of Contact discovery. A new contact enrichment setting lets you choose whether Prospecting should search for additional POCs such as business owners, managers, and key decision makers, turning a business listing into named people you can actually sell to.
  3. WhatsApp and SMS notifications for Rentals. Days after Rentals went live for every account on June 30, WhatsApp arrived as a notification channel across all Rentals event types, including unconfirmed, booked, canceled, rescheduled, reminder, and follow-up, with separate templates for the contact and the business, plus test sends before going live. SMS notifications for Rentals shipped the same day, configured under Calendar Settings.
  4. Faster prospect list building followed on July 2. Adding a prospect no longer forces a Marketing Audit Report to generate every time; a new toggle lets you save prospects in bulk first and run audits later for the highest-priority opportunities.
  5. A pricing footnote that matters. HighLevel also published updated WhatsApp per-message pricing effective with July 2026 billing, tracking Meta's rate card. UK marketing template messages, for example, rise from $0.0555 to $0.0667, while service conversations stay free in every market. HighLevel's own advice to resellers: review your markups for the changed markets.

What it means for operators

The enrichment update is the one to act on. The gap between a scraped business listing and a contactable lead has always been manual research time, and that gap is exactly what this closes. Agencies selling local lead generation should switch the enrichment setting on, turn on POC discovery, and rebuild their prospecting lists so every new record lands with a phone, an email, and a named owner or manager. That aligns with where outbound is heading in 2026: precision and verified data over raw volume. It also compounds with the free AI window from the Summer of AI program, which runs through August 31, so enriched records can flow straight into AI-handled first-touch conversations at no extra AI cost this summer.

The WhatsApp addition is smaller but real money for booking-heavy businesses: reminder messages on the channel clients actually open cut no-shows, and rentals operators get the full event lifecycle covered. Just watch the per-message rates if you resell, and reprice your communication bundles accordingly, a discipline that matters more as the whole stack goes metered, as we cover in today's cost playbook.

If you want a HighLevel build where prospecting, enrichment, AI first-touch, and booking notifications run as one pipeline, that is exactly what our GoHighLevel services team sets up, and it feeds directly into a broader lead generation system that fills your pipeline predictably.
faqs: What did GoHighLevel change about Prospecting on July 1, 2026?

Prospecting now enriches new prospects automatically in sub-account prospecting where available, scraping phone numbers and emails from the prospect's website, and a new setting lets it discover additional Points of Contact such as owners, managers, and key decision makers. Records arrive in the CRM outreach-ready.
What is the new POC discovery setting for?

It tells Prospecting to look beyond the business listing for named decision makers. For local lead generation, that converts a generic business record into specific people to contact, which supports the precision-over-volume outbound approach that is winning in 2026.
Which booking events can send WhatsApp notifications now?

All Rentals event types: unconfirmed, booked, canceled, rescheduled, reminder, and follow-up. You can set different WhatsApp templates for the contact and the business, and SMS notifications for Rentals shipped the same day through Calendar Settings.
Does the WhatsApp update change what agencies pay?

HighLevel has updated WhatsApp per-message pricing effective July 2026. Agencies that rebill WhatsApp usage to clients should recheck their markups and communication bundle pricing so the new rates do not silently eat their margin.
